playbooks.title: Opportunity Scan
playbooks.selection_hint: First step of validation. Surfaces and scores real evidence of demand. Run for every business.

playbooks.title: Problem Validation Interviews
playbooks.selection_hint: Run before building anything. Confirms the problem is real and urgent, not just plausible. Skip only if a strong review corpus already substitutes.

playbooks.title: Competitive Teardown
playbooks.selection_hint: Run for every business to find defensible whitespace. Not a feature checklist; a strategy and moat diagnosis.

playbooks.title: Market Sizing (TAM/SAM/SOM)
playbooks.selection_hint: Run for every business to confirm the opportunity is large enough to justify the build. Kill if SOM is below threshold.

playbooks.title: Jobs-to-be-Done Extraction
playbooks.selection_hint: Run when interview data or a review corpus exists. Feeds positioning, roadmap, and the competitive teardown. Needs source material to be useful.

playbooks.title: Red-Team the Thesis
playbooks.selection_hint: The L0 gate. Run before committing capital. Produces the validated thesis and kill criteria that unlock incorporation.

playbooks.title: Why-Now Memo
playbooks.selection_hint: Run when the thesis is articulated. Produces the timing narrative reused later in the fundraise deck. Skip if only validating internally.

playbooks.title: Beachhead Selection
playbooks.selection_hint: The first L1 commit. Choose one wedge so all force concentrates on a single point. Unlocks positioning and naming.

playbooks.title: Company Naming
playbooks.selection_hint: Run after the wedge is chosen so the name targets the right audience. The name is the most durable IP; do not rush it.

playbooks.title: Domain Acquisition
playbooks.selection_hint: Run once the name is final. Secures the primary digital identity. Money and broker negotiation make this a human-gated spend.

playbooks.title: Trademark Clearance
playbooks.selection_hint: Run the knockout search the moment the name is chosen; file once goods/services are defined. Filing is a legal, money, hard-to-reverse action.

playbooks.title: Entity Formation
playbooks.selection_hint: Run once the venture is committed and you need to bank, contract, hire, or raise. Default Delaware C-Corp; deviations need a documented reason.

playbooks.title: Founding Docs
playbooks.selection_hint: Run the moment the entity is registered; the 83(b) clock is 30 days. Errors here are fatal to future fundraising.

playbooks.title: ToS & Privacy Policy
playbooks.selection_hint: Required before any product that collects user data goes public. Gates on a formed entity and known data practices. Legal and irreversible; always human-gated.

playbooks.title: Brand Positioning Statement
playbooks.selection_hint: Strategic positioning bet that every content, brand, and trademark step downstream consumes. Run once the MVP slice is scoped.

playbooks.title: Visual Identity Brief
playbooks.selection_hint: At L3 ship only the Minimum Viable Brand (Inter, one primary color, text wordmark). The full identity here is a post-PMF mature variant; do not run the full build pre-PMF.

playbooks.title: Tone of Voice Guide
playbooks.selection_hint: The voice contract every content-producing agent reads. Run after positioning so all copy speaks consistently.

playbooks.title: MVP Scoping
playbooks.selection_hint: Defines the smallest buildable product after the thesis survives red-teaming. Gates tech stack, positioning, and PRD.

playbooks.title: Tech Stack Selection
playbooks.selection_hint: The infra gate. Pick the stack before hosting, observability, or any build can start. Skip only for businesses that ship no software.

playbooks.title: PRD Drafting
playbooks.selection_hint: The source of truth engineering and design agents execute against. Run per initiative once the MVP slice is scoped and validated.

playbooks.title: Feature Prioritization
playbooks.selection_hint: The standing decision of what to build next. Runs continuously through build and beyond, not once. Full scoring every two weeks; triage daily.

playbooks.title: Onboarding Flow Design
playbooks.selection_hint: The highest-leverage growth surface. Design it once the product core value and signup flow exist; it feeds the beta program.

playbooks.title: Usability Testing Protocol
playbooks.selection_hint: Behavioral testing of whether users can accomplish goals. Run on a stable prototype or staging build, repeatedly through build. Not for attitudinal questions.

playbooks.title: Beta Program Management
playbooks.selection_hint: PMF-validation and relationship-building, not QA. Run once the core workflow is functional and instrumented, ahead of public launch.

playbooks.title: Release & Changelog Cadence
playbooks.selection_hint: Decouples deploy from release and turns the changelog into a marketing, retention, and recruiting asset. Stands up once CI/CD exists; then operates continuously.

playbooks.title: Hosting & Deployment Setup
playbooks.selection_hint: Stands up the deploy foundation once the stack is chosen. Everything observability, security, backup, and release depends on this landing.

playbooks.title: Observability Setup
playbooks.selection_hint: Run once a deployed app exists. It is the sensory layer that arms incident response and tells you why something broke, not just that it broke.

playbooks.title: Incident Response
playbooks.selection_hint: Install once observability is live, then it runs always-on. Triggered by any alert, outage, or security event. Never a one-time checkbox.

playbooks.title: Security Baseline
playbooks.selection_hint: Stand up day one alongside hosting. Prevents the cheap, predictable breaches (stolen credentials dominate) and avoids expensive SOC 2 retrofits.

playbooks.title: Data Backup & Recovery
playbooks.selection_hint: Run once any data store exists. Turns an outage, ransomware event, or bad migration into a 30-minute problem instead of an existential one.

playbooks.title: Analytics Stack Setup
playbooks.selection_hint: The sensory apparatus of the business. Every downstream metric, funnel, cohort, and dashboard depends on it. Set up in week one to avoid an 18-month rebuild.

playbooks.title: Event Taxonomy Design
playbooks.selection_hint: Run right after the analytics stack lands. A disciplined taxonomy is what makes funnels, cohorts, and reads trustworthy; without it tracking drifts into chaos.

playbooks.title: North Star Metric Definition
playbooks.selection_hint: Define once early PMF signal exists so every agent optimizes one number. Before PMF, anchor it on retention or core-action completion, not growth.

playbooks.title: Funnel Analysis
playbooks.selection_hint: Run once events flow and there are users moving through the funnel. Diagnoses where the funnel leaks and produces testable remediation hypotheses.

playbooks.title: Cohort & Retention Analysis
playbooks.selection_hint: Run once a critical event and a usage interval are definable. Retention is the most leveraged metric; a 10% lift beats a 10% acquisition lift many times over.

playbooks.title: Attribution Modeling
playbooks.selection_hint: Run before scaling paid spend, once there is meaningful spend history and conversion volume. Attribution is causal inference, not a reporting exercise.

playbooks.title: CEO Dashboard Build
playbooks.selection_hint: Build once the NSM is defined and data sources are connected. Gives the founder one health screen that alerts on exception rather than being checked.

playbooks.title: GA4 Reading Playbook
playbooks.selection_hint: Run once GA4 (ideally with a BigQuery export) is live and there is traffic to read, typically from launch. Skip products that do not use GA4.

playbooks.title: Mixpanel Reading Playbook
playbooks.selection_hint: Run once Mixpanel has events flowing and a tracking plan exists, typically from launch. Skip products that do not use Mixpanel.

playbooks.title: Weekly Business Review
playbooks.selection_hint: Install once data flows and metrics have owners, then it runs always-on every week. The primary mechanism against operational drift. Never cancelled.

playbooks.title: Pricing Research
playbooks.selection_hint: Run before any packaging or pricing-page decision, once positioning and an ICP exist. Skip only for permanently-free products.

playbooks.title: Packaging & Tier Design
playbooks.selection_hint: Run after pricing research lands a price range and value metric. The structural decision that pricing-page copy and freemium choice both depend on.

playbooks.title: Pricing Page Copy & Layout
playbooks.selection_hint: Run once positioning and tiers are locked, never before, or the page gets rewritten around placeholder prices. The highest-intent page on the site.

playbooks.title: Freemium / Trial Decision
playbooks.selection_hint: Run after pricing research, before launch. Decides the shape of the acquisition funnel. Most relevant to self-serve software; less so to high-touch services.

playbooks.title: Discount & Promo Policy
playbooks.selection_hint: Install once a list price and gross margin exist, before the sales motion starts giving discounts. A standing guardrail, not a one-off campaign.

playbooks.title: Positioning Canvas
playbooks.selection_hint: The strategic root of all GTM. Run once a beachhead is committed; every pricing, launch, and content decision derives from it.

playbooks.title: Category Design Decision
playbooks.selection_hint: Run after positioning when the market frame is genuinely in question. Skip if the category is obvious and the play is clearly to win share in it.

playbooks.title: Launch Plan (T-90)
playbooks.selection_hint: The conductor of the launch level. Run once product, positioning, and pricing are ready; it sequences PH, HN, PR, and podcast plays under one countdown.

playbooks.title: Product Hunt Launch
playbooks.selection_hint: Run under the launch plan when the product is live with no waitlist and the audience is the tech community. Skip for local services and pure offline plays.

playbooks.title: Hacker News Launch
playbooks.selection_hint: Run under the launch plan only when the product appeals to a technical audience and the submitting account is aged. Skip for non-technical B2C.

playbooks.title: PR & Press Launch
playbooks.selection_hint: Run for any public announcement with a real narrative. Owned media leads; earned media amplifies. Hold wire distribution until Series A.

playbooks.title: Podcast Tour
playbooks.selection_hint: Run when a speaker has 4+ hours a week for 8 weeks and conversion infrastructure exists. A high-trust, zero-media-spend channel; not a branded podcast.

playbooks.title: Community Seeding
playbooks.selection_hint: Run from L3 when owned audience is under ~500 and a landing page or demo exists. Value-first participation; never a paid-ads or owned-community play.

playbooks.title: SEO Keyword Research
playbooks.selection_hint: Run before any content or SEO work. Foundation that every content and programmatic-SEO playbook consumes. Skip only if SEO is not a planned channel.

playbooks.title: Content Calendar
playbooks.selection_hint: The operating engine for content. Install once keywords and personas exist; then run continuously to plan, brief, publish, and refresh.

playbooks.title: Pillar + Cluster Content
playbooks.selection_hint: Use to build topical authority on a target domain. Run after keywords exist; produces the linkable long-form assets later channels and link-building depend on.

playbooks.title: Long-form Blog Post
playbooks.selection_hint: The primary organic acquisition unit. Run per keyword cluster once research exists; produces the linkable assets link-building later needs.

playbooks.title: Programmatic SEO
playbooks.selection_hint: Only when a keyword pattern has aggregate volume and a unique dataset can populate 100+ genuinely distinct pages. Scale only post-PMF; thin pages at scale risk sitewide deindexation.

playbooks.title: Technical SEO Audit
playbooks.selection_hint: Run quarterly, after migrations, after core updates, or on a traffic drop. Keeps the site crawlable and indexable so content and programmatic SEO can rank.

playbooks.title: Link Building
playbooks.selection_hint: Run once long-form linkable assets exist and a domain is warmed. Safe earlier, but compounds best post-PMF. Never outreach without a linkable asset to point at.

playbooks.title: Newsletter Growth
playbooks.selection_hint: The owned, algorithm-independent audience channel. Stand up once an ESP and authenticated domain exist; then run continuously. Other channels feed it.

playbooks.title: YouTube Strategy
playbooks.selection_hint: A compounding video channel where packaging and retention drive distribution. Run continuously if video fits the audience and the team can sustain production.

playbooks.title: TikTok Growth
playbooks.selection_hint: B2C / consumer channel. Short-form video where the first 3 seconds decide reach. Skip for pure B2B; run continuously when the audience is on TikTok.

playbooks.title: Twitter / X Content Engine
playbooks.selection_hint: Real-time audience and reply-surface channel; strong for founder-led and tech audiences. Run continuously where the ICP is active on X.

playbooks.title: LinkedIn Organic
playbooks.selection_hint: B2B channel. Founder-led authority content into a high-intent professional audience; feeds the dark funnel. Skip for pure B2C. Gates LinkedIn Ads (070) later.

playbooks.title: Instagram Growth
playbooks.selection_hint: B2C / consumer channel. Reels-first acquisition with DM funnels replacing link-in-bio. Skip for pure B2B; run continuously when the audience is on Instagram.

playbooks.title: Reddit Marketing
playbooks.selection_hint: Community-first channel with evergreen SEO and heavy AI-citation weight. Run continuously where the ICP has active subreddits. Requires a warmed account; never spam.

playbooks.title: Google Ads
playbooks.selection_hint: Run post-PMF once analytics, event taxonomy, and attribution exist. Demand capture for categories where buyers already search; needs conversion data to optimize.

playbooks.title: Meta Ads
playbooks.selection_hint: Run post-PMF once analytics, event taxonomy, and attribution exist, and Business Manager plus pixel/CAPI (EMQ high) are live. Strong for B2C/DTC volume.

playbooks.title: LinkedIn Ads
playbooks.selection_hint: Only with ACV >= $5k, budget >= $3k/mo, audience >= 50k, and CAPI. CPM economics are fatal below the ACV floor; a B2C or low-ACV company never runs this.

playbooks.title: Retargeting
playbooks.selection_hint: Run post-PMF once a live page has audience volume (>= 1000 visitors/30d) and the landing page is optimized. Harvests existing demand; never a brand-building channel.

playbooks.title: Creative Testing
playbooks.selection_hint: Run continuously alongside active paid social once CPA targets are set. Creative drives ~56% of paid ROI; volume and speed of iteration are the structural advantage.

playbooks.title: Landing Page CRO
playbooks.selection_hint: Run on a live page with traffic or qualitative signal. The pre-flight gate for paid: scaling spend onto an unoptimized page wastes budget.

playbooks.title: Affiliate Program Setup
playbooks.selection_hint: Gated on M1 retention >= 40%, 50+ paying customers, and a clean funnel. An affiliate program is a multiplier for existing momentum, never a primary early channel.

playbooks.title: Influencer Partnerships
playbooks.selection_hint: Run post-PMF once unit economics are validated and a conversion path exists. Strongest for B2C/DTC; the mechanism is trust transfer, so audience fit and authenticity beat reach.

playbooks.title: Integration & Co-marketing
playbooks.selection_hint: Run once you have 50+ closed-won accounts, engineering bandwidth, and CRM partner fields. Ecosystem-led growth compounds where paid cannot; B2B SaaS oriented.

playbooks.title: Referral Program
playbooks.selection_hint: Gated on PMF. Never run before retention proves out; a referral loop on a leaky bucket amplifies churn and burns treasury.

playbooks.title: Welcome Email Sequence
playbooks.selection_hint: Highest-leverage lifecycle asset. Run once deliverability is warmed and the aha event is instrumented; it escorts new users to activation.

playbooks.title: Nurture Campaign
playbooks.selection_hint: Run once a CRM, lead scoring, and a content library exist. Behavior-responsive education for prospects who have raised their hand but are not yet ready to buy.

playbooks.title: Win-back Campaign
playbooks.selection_hint: Run once you have customers with churn or dormancy events and warmed deliverability. Recovers former customers at 5 to 25x lower cost than new acquisition.

playbooks.title: Transactional Emails
playbooks.selection_hint: Run once the product has user accounts and deliverability is warmed. These are the highest-open emails you own; treat as product surface, not marketing.

playbooks.title: Email Deliverability Setup
playbooks.selection_hint: Critical-path L2 item. Start day one because the warmup window is 30 to 45 days; every email playbook (078/079/080/081/084) is blocked until this is warmed.

playbooks.title: ICP & Target Account Listing
playbooks.selection_hint: Run before any outbound for a sales-led or hybrid B2B motion. Skip for pure B2C or self-serve products with no named-account targeting. Refresh on ICP drift (CAC up, churn up, win rate down).

playbooks.title: Outbound Email Sequences
playbooks.selection_hint: The outbound engine for a B2B sales-led motion. Needs warmed sending domains (082) and a target account list (083). Skip for pure B2C or self-serve.

playbooks.title: Discovery Call Framework
playbooks.selection_hint: The first substantive sales conversation in a B2B sales-led motion. Fires when a prospect accepts a meeting. Skip for pure B2C or self-serve with no human sales call.

playbooks.title: Demo Script
playbooks.selection_hint: The persuasion event after discovery qualifies a B2B deal. Fires only when discovery is complete and a sandbox is ready. Skip for pure B2C or self-serve with no live demo.

playbooks.title: Objection Handling Library
playbooks.selection_hint: A callable, always-on library invoked by discovery (085), demo (086), and close (088) whenever a prospect resists. Install once a B2B sales motion exists. Not run standalone.

playbooks.title: Contract & Close
playbooks.selection_hint: The commercial/legal close for a B2B deal, triggered by a verbal yes. Skip for pure B2C or self-serve checkout. The binding contract is a human gate.

playbooks.title: Support Workflow
playbooks.selection_hint: Install the moment the first paying customer exists. Applies to any business with customers, B2B or B2C. Operate continuously, not a one-time checkbox.

playbooks.title: Churn Diagnosis & Win-back
playbooks.selection_hint: Install once there is recurring revenue and billing webhooks. Highest-ROI lever is recovering involuntary (failed-payment) churn. Operate continuously.

playbooks.title: NPS / CSAT Program
playbooks.selection_hint: Install once there are active customers and a survey channel. Applies to any business, B2B or B2C. Operate on a fixed cadence; promoters feed the case-study pipeline.

playbooks.title: Case Study & Testimonial Pipeline
playbooks.selection_hint: Install once customers have measurable outcomes and an NPS signal exists. Applies to any business, B2B or B2C. Publishing a customer's story requires their consent.

playbooks.title: A/B Testing Protocol
playbooks.selection_hint: Activate once a surface has live traffic (about 100+ users/day). Below that, use qualitative learning instead, not A/B tests.

playbooks.title: Growth Loop Design
playbooks.selection_hint: Run first in scale acquisition, before scaling paid spend. Needs market-product fit and channel data already in hand.

playbooks.title: Pricing and Packaging Experimentation
playbooks.selection_hint: Run when there are live paying customers (about 100+ willingness-to-pay respondents reachable). Highest-leverage profit lever, but every change passes a founder gate.

playbooks.title: Financial Model and Forecast
playbooks.selection_hint: Run when 12 to 24 months of actuals exist and a raise or board cadence is near. Consumes unit economics; produces the model the fundraise leans on.

playbooks.title: Unit Economics
playbooks.selection_hint: Baseline at L5 once customer-level billing and channel spend exist. The single answer to "do we make money on one customer?" Feeds the model and the raise.

playbooks.title: Fundraise Deck and Investor Outreach
playbooks.selection_hint: Only when raising. Needs PMF or a real milestone, a defensible model, and case studies. Skip entirely if staying unfunded.

playbooks.title: Human-in-the-Loop Approval Gates
playbooks.selection_hint: Installed day zero before Level 0, before any agent takes any action. The safety wrapper around every other playbook. Never skip.

playbooks.title: LLM Cost and Model Routing
playbooks.selection_hint: Installed day zero before Level 0, right after HITL gates. Every LLM call passes through it. A misconfigured loop can burn five figures in minutes.

playbooks.title: Marketplace Trust and Safety
playbooks.selection_hint: Run before opening a marketplace to real transactions. Liquidity without trust is churn; the first bad transaction without a dispute path is a dead marketplace.

playbooks.title: Marketplace Liquidity Balancing
playbooks.selection_hint: Run once the marketplace has live transactions and a measurable match rate. The single most important marketplace growth lever; balance the short side before spending on the long side.

playbooks.title: Local Google Business Profile and Maps
playbooks.selection_hint: Run early for any local service. For a local business this is the primary discovery channel, ahead of a website or paid ads.

playbooks.title: Local Reviews and Reputation
playbooks.selection_hint: Run once a local service has repeat customers. Reviews are the compounding local-ranking and trust lever; start the loop as early as you have happy customers.

playbooks.title: B2B Security and SOC2 Readiness
playbooks.selection_hint: Run when enterprise deals start stalling on security review, or before moving upmarket. Security review is a gate on B2B revenue once deal sizes grow.

playbooks.title: App Store Optimization and Mobile Growth
playbooks.selection_hint: Run for a consumer mobile app around launch. The store listing is the top of the mobile funnel; optimize it before spending on installs.

playbooks.title: Pre-seed Fundraise Narrative
playbooks.selection_hint: Run early for a founder who intends to raise, before revenue or PMF exist. The pre-seed raise sells the team and the insight, not metrics; do not wait for traction the round does not require.
